operating_state: Revenue increased 38% to $15.6 billion driven by strong volume growth in incretin medicines Mounjaro and Zepbound, partially offset by lower realized prices. Mounjaro revenue reached $5.2 billion with 68% growth, while Zepbound revenue surged 172% to $3.4 billion, both reflecting robust demand despite pricing pressures. Verzenio grew 12% to $1.5 billion driven by volume expansion across regions.
cost_structure: Gross margin expanded to 84.3% from 80.8% driven by improved production costs and favorable product mix, offsetting lower realized prices. Research and development expenses increased 23% reflecting continued late-stage portfolio investments. Marketing, selling, and administrative expenses rose 30% supporting ongoing and future product launches.
material_events: Acquired Scorpion's PI3Kα inhibitor program STX-478 for $1.86 billion in acquired IPR&D charges. Issued $6.5 billion in fixed-rate debt to fund acquisitions and general business purposes. Completed $1.89 billion in share repurchases and paid $2.69 billion in dividends. Multiple regulatory submissions and approvals across tirzepatide, orforglipron, and other pipeline assets.
macro_exposures: Presidential administration issued executive order targeting most-favored-nation pricing with September 2025 deadline for manufacturer alignment. OBBBA enacted implementing healthcare spending cuts and tax changes. Ongoing tariff investigations on pharmaceutical imports may result in sector-specific restrictions. IRA government pricing selections expected to expand beyond current Jardiance inclusion.
forward_outlook: Tirzepatide supply now exceeds U.S. demand with additional manufacturing capacity coming online over several years. Executed supply agreements requiring up to $8 billion in purchase commitments over 8-year terms. Capital expenditures expected to increase meaningfully supporting global manufacturing expansion. OBBBA expected to increase effective tax rate to approximately 19% for 2025.
uncertainty_profile: Counterfeit and compounded incretin production continues despite FDA shortage declaration ending. Regulatory focus on business combinations may delay or increase costs of development activities. Foreign currency exposure primarily against euro, yen, and yuan with hedging providing partial mitigation. Pending litigation and investigations related to 340B program, insulin access, and pricing matters.
